---
title: "Module Library"
---

# Reusable teaching modules

These pages **follow the same story as the slides**, but you can read them slowly on the website: good for **before class**, **catching up after a missed day**, or **review** before an exam.

We use **two kinds of examples**: **simulated genes** (we wrote the rules, so we know what is true) and **[Palmer Penguins](../dataset-penguins.qmd)** (real measurements you can plot and argue about).

| Module | Day focus | In plain language |
|--------|-----------|-------------------|
| [01 — Foundations](#module-01-foundations-and-generalization) | Monday | Train vs test, “too wiggly” models, one-page **data description** habit |
| [02 — Regularization](#module-02-regularization) | Monday | Ridge, lasso, and stepwise/AIC — when to shrink coefficients or drop predictors |
| [03 — Trees & cultures](#module-03-trees-and-two-cultures) | Tuesday | Two ways of thinking about statistics + **decision trees** (“if–then” rules) |
| [04 — Modeling pipeline in R](#module-04-tidymodels-pipeline) | Tuesday | **Same step-by-step code as Tuesday’s slides**: preprocess, fit, tune, and plot a penguin classifier (uses the `tidymodels` packages) |
| [05 — Advanced models](#module-05-advanced-models) | Thursday | Stronger “black box” models (random forest, boosting, neural nets) — **same checking discipline**, bigger capacity |
| [06 — Metrics & explanations](#module-06-metrics-imbalance-and-shap) | Thursday | Picking scores that match the biology/clinic question, imbalance, and reading **SHAP** plots carefully |

---

## Module 01: Foundations and Generalization {#module-01-foundations-and-generalization}

**Predicting an outcome from measurements**, splitting data so you are honest about performance, and the “too simple vs too wiggly” picture you see on Monday. Links **gene simulations** and **penguins** so the same ideas appear on two tables.

**[Open Module 01 →](module-01-foundations.qmd)**

---

## Module 02: Regularization {#module-02-regularization}

**Ridge** and **lasso** gently pull coefficients toward zero; **AIC / stepwise** search different subsets of predictors. Connects Monday’s slides to the **gene LASSO** notebook and **penguin body-mass** shrinkage practice.

**[Open Module 02 →](module-02-regularization.qmd)**

---

## Module 03: Trees and Two Cultures {#module-03-trees-and-two-cultures}

Two traditions in statistics (interpretable models vs flexible prediction), then **decision trees**: sequential splits, depth, and what a **tree diagram** means for penguins.

**[Open Module 03 →](module-03-trees-and-cultures.qmd)**

---

## Module 04: Modeling pipeline in R {#module-04-tidymodels-pipeline}

The **same worked example as Tuesday’s deck**: Adelie vs Gentoo penguins, with **plots after tuning** (accuracy-type scores, variable importance, tree picture, ROC curve). All code lives in one shared file so slides and this page **never drift apart**.

**[Open Module 04 →](module-04-pipeline.qmd)**

---

## Module 05: Advanced models {#module-05-advanced-models}

Random forests, boosting, and neural nets as **more flexible** learners. Emphasis: keep **cross-validation and metrics** strict, and **swap the type of model** in the same overall workflow you learned on Tuesday.

**[Open Module 05 →](module-05-advanced-models.qmd)**

---

## Module 06: Metrics, imbalance, and SHAP {#module-06-metrics-imbalance-and-shap}

Choosing **numbers that match the decision** (miss a case vs call a false alarm), **class imbalance**, and using **SHAP** and importance plots as **descriptions** — not as proof you should intervene in a biological system.

**[Open Module 06 →](module-06-evaluation-and-interpretability.qmd)**
